title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Desmond and Nicholas, two young men helping at a party, show Hercule Poirot distorted photographs they created using makeup and different hairstyles to act as "imagined husbands" for the girls. They used lighting and mirrors to project these images, a setup that pleased Mrs. Drecker. Desmond notes that during the afternoon, several people were present, including Mrs. Drecker, Mrs. Butler, a school teacher named Miss Whitaker, Mrs. Flettbard, and Miss Lee, the pharmacist. The Reynolds children were also there, including the victim, Joyce Reynolds, her sister Anne, and a boy named Leopold, alongside Beatrice Adler and Kathy Grant. Men present included the vicar and a stuttering assistant vicar.

When Poirot asks about Joyce Reynolds, Nicholas mentions a rumor that she claimed to have seen a murder through extrasensory perception shortly before her death. Desmond and Nicholas deny hearing this directly, as they were busy installing lights and decorations. Desmond suggests Miss Whitaker as a suspect, citing her perceived sexual frustration and a past incident involving a strangled teacher. Nicholas suggests that Miss Emery might know more about Whitaker's character. Desmond also speculates that the assistant curate might have killed Joyce in a ritualistic manner involving apples.

Later, Poirot speaks with Miss Goodbody, a pleasant woman with a witch-like face. She explains that she writes poems using the girls' baptismal names, which are read aloud by girls imitating spirits during the mirror illusion. She observes the changing fashions of the young people and reflects on historical clothing styles.
